whatThisIs: Show the Tactical Combat Casualty Care (TCCC) tourniquet and wound-packing reference card: limb tourniquet application high and tight above the wound until bleeding stops, tightening windlass until distal pulse is absent and time-of-application written on the strap; junctional and torso wounds packed with hemostatic-impregnated gauze (Combat Gauze or equivalent) under three minutes of direct pressure; reassessment, conversion to pressure dressing when tactically feasible, and documentation of placement time so the receiving facility can decide on conversion vs. continued occlusion.
whenToUse: Use this in pre-hospital trauma when massive external hemorrhage from a compressible site is the immediate threat to life, in austere settings (combat, mass-casualty, prolonged-field-care, wilderness) where definitive surgical control is delayed, or when teaching the MARCH algorithm (Massive hemorrhage, Airway, Respiration, Circulation, Hypothermia/Head) sequence. The card is the technique reference - patient selection, the decision to apply, and the conversion decision still rest with the provider on scene.
